lessons.title: 11.0: MY SCHOOL
topics: This topic is essential for learners as it provides foundational vocabulary and
expressions related to daily life in a school setting. By exploring classroom orders and
instructions, learners will learn how to navigate and communicate effectively in a
French speaking classroom environment. Understanding subjects and school time

lessons.title: 12.0 DAILY ACTIVITIES
activities: Overview
learners, regardless of their cultural background. This topic lays a foundation for
everyday communication and helps learners to gain confidence in daily conversations,
to use key verbs and vocabulary while understanding the rhythm of daily life in
French-speaking cultures. Thus, this topic will help learners connect the language to
their real-life experiences and promote a more natural and practical use of French.
The topic includes one subtopic: Telling Time in French.
General Competences
assessment: learning activities from all the sub-topics taught and the summative
i. Vocabulary for daily activities in French.

lessons.title: 13.0 THE PAST
topics: The past refers to a time that has already happened, before the present moment. It
could be recent (yesterday, last week) or distant (years ago). The past usually means
past tense and it is used to talk about actions or events that have already happened. In
French, learners start by learning pass√© compose which is the most commonly used
